constraints: Must be O(1) or O(log n) average for get and put.
Do not use OrderedDict or built-in LRU cache.
disallowed_assumptions: All keys are accessed at least once.
expected_failure_modes: Evicting wrong item when frequencies are equal.
Resetting frequency on put of existing key incorrectly.
prohibited_claims: Model superiority — not authorized by this task.
Wrapper improvement without paired comparison — not measured.
prompt:
Implement a class `BoundedCache` with:
- `__init__(self, capacity: int)` — raises ValueError if capacity < 1.
- `get(self, key: str) -> object | None` — returns value or None.
- `put(self, key: str, value: object) -> None` — inserts or updates.

Eviction policy when at capacity:
1. Prefer to evict items with the lowest access frequency.
2. Among items with the same frequency, evict the least recently used.
3. On `put` of an existing key, update the value and reset its frequency to 1.
4. On `get` of an existing key, increment its frequency by 1.

Handle: capacity=1, updating existing keys, repeated gets on the same key, putting None as a value (valid), key with empty string (valid).
public_contract: BoundedCache.get(key) -> object | None; BoundedCache.put(key, value) -> None
scoring_focus: Updating existing key resets frequency to 1.
title: Bounded Cache Eviction Logic
